_info: This file contains all prompts used by vaultmind. Edit freely — no Python knowledge needed. Fields starting with underscore (_) are comments and are ignored by the scripts.
_tip: Use {placeholders} carefully — they are replaced by the scripts at runtime. Do not remove them. Literal curly braces that are NOT placeholders must be written as {{ and }} so Python does not confuse them with placeholders.
grounding: IMPORTANT: Only use information explicitly present in the notes above. Do not infer, invent, or assume anything not directly written. If something is unclear, say so.
_grounding_tip: The grounding rule is injected into every prompt. Lower it if you want more creative output. Remove it entirely at your own risk — the model will hallucinate more.

insights._info: Used by generate_insights.py. Runs once per week or month depending on DAYS_BACK in config.py.

lenses._info: Each lens is a separate AI call. Add, remove or edit lenses freely. The script picks them all up automatically.
lenses.name: Therapist
lenses.instruction: Focus on emotional patterns, mood shifts, mental health signals, stress, and psychological well-being. Be compassionate but honest.

lenses.name: Coach
lenses.instruction: Focus on productivity, goals mentioned, tasks completed or stalled, habits, obstacles, and momentum. Be direct.

lenses.name: Pattern Detector
lenses.instruction: Look for recurring phrases, cycles, excuses, or deferred intentions (e.g. 'tomorrow', 'next week', 'when I...'). Identify loops the person might not see.

lenses.name: Strengths
lenses.instruction: Focus only on genuine wins, growth, resilience, and positives — even small ones the person may have mentioned casually without giving themselves credit.

lenses.name: Connections
lenses.instruction: Find links between notes that seem unrelated. Identify threads, ideas that reappear, or topics that might be influencing each other. IMPORTANT: every note reference MUST be formatted as a wikilink [[NoteName]] using the exact filename without extension. Never reference a note as plain text — always [[NoteName]].

insights.synthesis_prompt:
You have analyzed someone's personal Obsidian notes from the past {period} through 5 different lenses:

{combined}

{grounding}

Now write a final synthesis of 150-200 words covering:
1. The single most important pattern or insight across all lenses
2. One honest challenge or blind spot
3. One concrete thing worth trying in the coming week

Be direct, warm, and specific. Only reference things that were actually mentioned in the analyses above.

morning_briefing._info: Used by morning_briefing.py. Runs daily, reads notes modified in the last 24h.
morning_briefing.prompt:
You are generating a personal morning briefing for someone's Obsidian vault.
Today is {weekday}, {date}.

{grounding}

NOTES MODIFIED YESTERDAY:
{yesterday_block}

NOTES MODIFIED TODAY (so far):
{today_block}

Write a concise morning briefing in Markdown with these sections:

## ☀️ Good Morning
One warm, motivating sentence based on what they were working on. Be specific, not generic.

## 📝 Yesterday's Activity
What did they work on? Reference specific note names. 2-4 bullet points max.

## ⚠️ Pending & Unfinished
Any tasks, todos, or unfinished items found in the notes. Use - [ ] format.
If nothing found, write "Nothing pending found in notes."

## 🎯 Suggested Focus for Today
Based on what's in progress, suggest 1-3 things worth tackling today. Be specific.

## 📅 Notes Modified
List all note filenames that were modified as [[wikilinks]].
IMPORTANT: format every single note as [[NoteName]] without the .md extension. Never write plain filenames.

Be concise, warm, and specific. Always write in English, regardless of the language of the notes.
morning_briefing._prompt_tip: Placeholders: {weekday}, {date}, {grounding}, {yesterday_block}, {today_block}. Change 'Always write in English' to match your preferred language.

study_recap._info: Used by study_recap.py. Run after a study session to generate key concepts and review questions.
study_recap.prompt:
You are creating a study recap for spaced repetition review.

{grounding}

STUDIED NOTES:
{notes_block}

ALL VAULT NOTE TITLES (for finding connections):
{all_titles}

Generate a structured study recap in Markdown with these sections:

## 📚 Key Concepts
For each studied note, list the most important concepts, definitions, and ideas.
Use sub-headings per note. Be specific — extract actual content, not vague descriptions.
Use [[NoteName]] wikilinks when referencing the studied notes.

## ❓ Review Questions
Write 5-10 questions that test understanding of the material studied.
Mix easy recall questions with deeper "explain why" or "what's the difference between" questions.
Format as a numbered list. Do NOT include answers.

## 🔗 Connections to Other Notes
Identify 2-5 genuine connections between the studied material and other notes in the vault.
Format each as: [[StudiedNote]] -> [[OtherNote]]: one sentence explaining the connection.
Only include connections that are genuinely meaningful, not superficial.

Write in the same language as the studied notes. Be specific and dense — this is for studying, not casual reading.
study_recap._prompt_tip: Placeholders: {grounding}, {notes_block}, {all_titles}. Change '5-10 questions' to adjust review question count.

txt_to_notes._info: Used by txt_to_notes.py. Converts any text file into structured Obsidian notes. Two-pass process: plan then write.
txt_to_notes._plan_prompt_tip: Placeholders: {instructions_block}, {grounding}, {text}, {tags_hint}, {titles_hint}. The {{ and }} in the example are escaped braces — they render as { and } to the model.
